name: Voter Action Project Team
subject: Privatization doesn't help Americans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Seriously, folks -- Privatizing the USPS doesn't help Americans, it will harm rural communities all over the country, and it will remove a vital public service from our everyday lives. In addition, it'll destroy a safe, secure, and widely-used method of voting that is critical for those rural communities, for the disabled, and for our senior citizens.
We won't let them get away with this, but to fight back we need resources. RUSH $15 or more right now to protect mail-in voting, defend the USPS, and fight for the public services we need most >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=102625_3&amount=15]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
WaPo - Opinion: A frantic warning from 100 leading experts: Our democracy is in grave danger [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=102625_3&amount=25]
We’ll keep this short, Team: those experts are right.
The attacks of the last few years in our state legislatures across the country targeting the right to vote, especially minority communities, are an assault on our democracy.
Now Trump, Musk, DOGE, and the MAGA movement want to privatize our postal service and harm mail-in voting , which has helped millions of Americans vote -- not just in the pandemic, but going back to the states that adopted the widespread practice in the early 90's.
“ Our entire democracy is now at risk ,” they wrote. “ History will judge what we do at this moment. ”
With so much at stake now -- not just with voting rights -- we've got to push our efforts to fight back into overdrive ASAP.
Team, none of this happens without everyone doing their share. Fuel our work with $15 or whatever you can today to stop the destruction of our government, save our democracy, and protect the right to vote >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=102625_3&amount=15]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=102625_3&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=102625_3&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=102625_3&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=102625_3&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=102625_3&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=102625_3&amount=35]

https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=102625_3&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=102625_3&amount=20]

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 10/26/2025
body:
Parents vs. unions: The battle over Colorado’s schools
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**October 26, 2025** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST**
| | Dear Readers,
This week at **Rocky Mountain Voice** , our 12-part investigation—**The Douglas County Dirty Dozen** —exposed how activist candidates, union money, and national politics are aligning to capture school board seats.
**We uncovered:**
* **Day 4:** Kyrzia Parker, a self-described [“kink-allied therapist,”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) promotes drag shows, “all-ages” Pride Fests, and woke baby books.
* **Day 5:** The “[Community Choice Slate](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)”—four union-backed candidates endorsed by Planned Parenthood and the Socialist Working Families Party.
* **Day 6:** The American Federation of Teachers, led by Randy Weingarten, is funding “Community Voice” candidates [to advance DEI](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) mandates and gender policies.

**Ballots are out, and the countdown is on.** The next nine days will decide the future of Douglas County schools.
📲 _Catch up on all six episodes on_ [Instagram](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), [Facebook](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), [X (Twitter)](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), _or_ [TikTok](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
But this fight isn’t just about Douglas County — it’s happening across the state. When elected office is used to enforce ideology, Coloradans pay the price.
True public service puts children, families, and communities first — not unions, parties, or personal agendas.
In **Mesa County** , [families are standing firm](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) against Denver unions pouring thousands into local school races. Their message is simple: schools belong to parents, not agendas.
In **Aurora** , Councilwoman **Danielle Jurinsky** [is doing what others won’t](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)—confronting violent gangs, challenging state denial, and proving that courage still counts in public service.
In **Monument** , [parents are rallying behind](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) **Ginger Schaaf** , a mom and former athlete running to protect girls’ sports, academic integrity, and parental rights in D38.
Nearly 40 counties have joined the bipartisan **Fund It or Fix It** campaign, backed by lawmakers **Rick Taggart, Janice Rich, and Matt Soper**. Their warning: [unfunded state mandates](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) are draining local budgets and eroding trust in government.
And while some politicians defend a [government shutdown as “leverage](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.),” Rep. **Jeff** **Hurd** [chose service over self](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) — refusing his own pay until the budget is restored, while earning **President Trump’s endorsement** and passage of his first bill.
Meanwhile, the [state’s defiance](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) over wolf reintroduction, [Denver’s fiscal chaos](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), and the **NEA’s partnerships**[with pro-violence activists ](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)all point to the same problem — leaders who’ve forgotten who they serve.
Public service should never be about power — it should be about people. It’s time to rebuild leadership that listens, works, and delivers — rooted in accountability, not ideology.
Every ballot cast this week carries more than a vote — it carries a message:
👉 **Colorado still belongs to its people.**
In liberty and truth,
Team RMV
---
| | |
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[Parents vs. unions: The battle over Mesa County’s schools](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY KENT ZOOK, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _The same people and organizations trying to replace District 51 School board members, Andrea Haitz, Will Jones, and Angela Lema are the ones responsible for neglecting our schools in the first place. Previous board leadership allowed facilities to deteriorate to the point that repair costs became unfeasible._
**[Feds to Polis administration: Stop importing wolves from Canada](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Efforts by Colorado Parks and Wildlife to bring in more wolves from Canada later this year may have hit a snag after the U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service told the state it may not do so. In an Oct. 10 letter, Brian Nesvik, director of USFWS, told Gov. Jared Polis and Jeff Davis, director of Colorado Parks and Wildlife (CPW), that Colorado is not allowed to bring in gray wolves from Canada or Alaska.
**[Rep. Hurd refuses pay, passes first bill and earns Trump endorsement in decisive week](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) President Trump’s “Complete and Total Endorsement” of Congressman Jeff Hurd on Saturday capped a remarkable week for the freshman lawmaker from Colorado’s Third District. Trump praised Hurd‘s “strong Record of SUCCESS,” for “fighting tirelessly to... Advance American Energy DOMINANCE, Grow the Economy, Cut Taxes and Regulations...” and “HE WILL NOT LET YOU DOWN.”
|
---
**STATE**
**[Tina Peters’ attorney presses Governor Polis and Secretary Griswold to eliminate computer voting machines](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Attorney John Case, who represents former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters, has sent an open letter to Colorado Gov. Jared Polis and Secretary of State Jena Griswold urging the state to immediately discontinue electronic voting systems and return to in-person, hand-counted paper ballots.
**[Colorado Wolf Reintroduction May Have Violated Federal Law](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [CBS COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A plan to import 15 more wolves to Colorado from Canada may violate federal law. Wolves are listed as an endangered species here, so Colorado Parks and Wildlife needs a permit from the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service to relocate wolves here from other states. The permit issued by USFWS in 2023 lists six states where Colorado can source wolves. It doesn't list Canada, where CPW got 15 wolves last year and plans to get another 15 wolves this year.
**[Five Suspects Nabbed in Organized Crime Spree Across 25 Jurisdictions](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [FOX31](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The Boulder County Sheriff’s Office Wednesday morning executed nine search warrants after an 18-month investigation of an alleged criminal enterprise that committed crimes in 25 Colorado jurisdictions, resulting in the arrest of five people. One person remains at large. The 20th Judicial District Attorney’s Office said that the crime ring is responsible for over $828,880 in stolen property and $39,880 in damaged property, impacting more than 55 victims.
_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[Democrat Whip Defends Using Shutdown as “Leverage” While Families Struggle](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE POST MILLENNIAL](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Congresswoman Katherine Clark, the Democratic whip in the House of Representatives, said that although families are suffering due to the government shutdown, the ongoing dispute is a time of political “leverage” for her party.
**[Teachers Union Criticized for Letting Radical Groups Shape Curriculum](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [WASHINGTON EXAMINER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Teaching resources authored by Hamas sympathizers, praise for violent left-wing activists, and activities where students roleplay as “queer liberation” activists fighting against the “Religious Right” represent just a small sample of the material produced by the Zinn Education Project and its parent organization, Rethinking Schools.
**[Lawmakers Push Citizenship Requirement After Startling Voter Roll Discoveries](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [JUST THE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The dual revelations that an illegal alien school administrator from Iowa made it onto the voter rolls in Maryland and that over 2,700 noncitizens escaped strict voter ID checks to qualify to vote in Texas are creating new momentum for creating a nationwide citizenship check system for elections.
**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Johnstown Breeze publisher under fire for “Mexican whipping” email to Weld County GOP](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY RMV STAFF, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The publisher of the Johnstown Breeze signs her emails with a Dalai Lama quote: “Be kind whenever possible. It is always possible.” But her recent message to the Weld County Republican Party was anything but kind.
**[Denver’s Budget Crisis Deepens Amid $2 Million Rent Default](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [CBS COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The city of Denver’s largest tenant, the leaseholder for the Denver Post building at 101 West Colfax Avenue, stopped making its monthly $650,000 rent payments to the city in August. And with late charges, the leaseholder is now about $2 million behind in rent, according to an exclusive CBS News Colorado investigation.
**[‘No Kings’ 2.0 Protest Brings Arrests and Tighter Security](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Denver police on Sunday morning confirmed authorities arrested 12 people during follow-up demonstrations Saturday, after tens of thousands flooded downtown streets for the “No Kings” afternoon protests.
**_[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[Colorado must stop pushing unfunded laws on local governments, lawmakers say](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [DAILY SENTINEL](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _When lawmakers pass a new bill, there’s one question we should all ask before we vote: Who’s going to pay for it? Too often, that question goes unanswered. Across Colorado, local governments are being asked to carry out new state laws — on everything from wildfire codes to building standards — without the funding to make them possible._
**[In Aurora, Danielle Jurinsky doesn’t talk change—she works for it](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY SHAINA COLE,[ ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Since taking office in 2021, Danielle Jurinsky has thrown herself into Aurora’s challenges. She’s backed measures to curb crime and pushed to ease the load on small businesses and residents when it comes to local taxes._

**[Tri-Lakes parents back Ginger Schaaf for D38 School Board—and stronger schools](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY AMY STEPHENS,[ ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Two years ago, Ginger Schaaf and her family moved to Monument after her husband retired from military service. Having lived in Olympia, Washington, they were ready to leave behind “woke” policies that made it untenable to stay. “It was so extreme that at local sports games there wasn’t even an American flag,” Ginger recalled._

**[The Shoes of Peace: Walking in Peace](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY DRAKE HUNTER,[ ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Last time, we talked about putting on The Shoes of Peace—being ready. But shoes aren’t just for standing still or being set, like in the phrase “Ready, set...” They’re for moving forward. They’re for GOING! This week, we’re lacing up and stepping into what it means to walk in peace. Let’s GO!_
**[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** Email [submit@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:submit@rockymountainvoice.com).
|
---
📚**DID YOU KNOW?** **School boards make up the largest group of elected officials in the U.S. — over 61,000 members across 13,000 districts!** Despite their massive influence over budgets, curriculum, and hiring, most serve as unpaid volunteers—and often only 5–10% of voters turn out in school board elections, meaning a handful of votes can truly shape a community’s future.
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** "Bad officials are elected by good citizens who don’t vote." – George Jean Nathan
|

The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | QUOTE OF THE DAY** "Bad officials are elected by good citizens who don’t vote." – George Jean Nathan | | | | ---|---|--- Did a friend forward you this email? | A product of Rocky Mountain Voice, Inc. 8200 S Quebec St, STE A3601 Centennial, CO 80112 This message was originally sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com Be sure to put “RockyMountainVoice.com” on your safe-senders whitelist. If you ever stop receiving our emails, it might be because someone to whom you forwarded the email unintentionally removed you from the list. No worries; it is easy enough to reactivate your subscription immediately by visiting: Before you click the link below... If someone forwarded this email to you, please don’t! Clicking the link will end the subscription of dpwillis67@gmail.com

name: Team DNC
subject: How about a little hope?
body:
---
Derek, Is there anything we can say to convince you to [**donate to the DNC today?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-october-2025?refcode=em_20251024_4999_fr&amount=7&refcode2=4999_22930333_gcYRNZ&akid=4999%2E22930333%2EgcYRNZ) **What if we shared what’s giving us hope right now?** With under two weeks left until Election Day in Virginia, more than **_836,000 voters**_ have cast their ballots and made their voices heard. That’s 269,000 more than were cast by this time in the 2021 election. In states like Virginia and New Jersey, we have a chance to send a powerful message at the polls — that people all over the country are fed up with Trump and his allies’ agenda. That’s the message we’ve sent all year, as Democrats have won or overperformed in nearly every key election this year by 15 points on average, many in some of the reddest states across the country. If there’s one thing that tells us, it’s that voters _know_ what’s at stake this year — and that’s all we need to keep us going. **If these trends continue, we have a real chance of winning key races in Virginia, California, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and more on Election Day.** At the DNC, we’re using these bits of hope amid the chaos to fuel our fight in these final weeks before Election Day. It’s all-hands-on-deck to reach as many voters as possible, to protect ballot access, persuade folks who may still be undecided, and make sure voters have a plan to make their voices heard. **Derek, this work is only possible with the help of grassroots supporters just like you.** If you’re looking to join the thousands of grassroots donors who have pitched in to fund our work this year, now is the perfect time. [**It’s going to take all of us building on these wins and signs of hope to create the powerful change we want to see. Will you join us and pitch in $7 to the DNC today so we can elect Democrats up and down the ballot this year and beyond?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-october-2025?refcode=em_20251024_4999_fr&amount=7&refcode2=4999_22930333_gcYRNZ&akid=4999%2E22930333%2EgcYRNZ) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-october-2025?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20251024_4999_fr&refcode2=4999_22930333_gcYRNZ&akid=4999%2E22930333%2EgcYRNZ)**

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_gen-election-update-em_q42025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_gen-election-update-em_q42025]
Sorry to email you so late, friend.
I’m up thinking about our race. I’ve got so many questions racing through my head, like:
Do people know that Election Day is in just 9 days?
Do voters know that my opponent, Jason Miyares, is fully endorsed by Donald Trump and his MAGA extremist allies?
Do folks realize that our race for Attorney General is a dead heat, and their support could be the difference between us winning and losing?
There’s so much at stake in this race. And it’s up to us to win on November 4th.
We'll need to do everything we can by keeping our ads live, passing out yard signs, and creating a huge voter organizing effort to defeat Jason Miyares on November 4th.
So with just a few days left, will you chip in $9 (or more!) to help us keep things running at full speed before Election Day? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_gen-election-update-em_q42025?amount=9]

name: AOC
subject: A radical and outlandish act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
We must remember in times such as this: we are not the crazy ones.
#
It has always been in our nation’s darkest chapters that our brightest lights emerge.
And we — each and every one of us, whether we like it not — are living history today.
From our nation’s beginning, American democracy has always reflected the struggle between humanity’s highest and lowest natures: between subjects and citizens, the Confederacy and the Union, freedom and bondage, labor and capital rights and regimes.
**It is no different today**.
The struggle of our time today is the single most unjust concentration of wealth in the hands of the very few, unseen in our nation’s and the world’s history.
**It is a time in which demanding what should be considered the most basic tenets of human dignity is considered a radical and outlandish act**.
It is a time such as today, when demanding affordable housing and decent wages is considered a radical and outlandish act.
That we could afford our lives, our groceries, our transit? Is considered a radical and outlandish act.
That we accept our neighbor as ourselves? A radical and outlandish act.
We must remember in times such as this: **we are not the crazy ones**. We are not the outlandish ones.
They want us to think we are crazy — we are sane.
To demand affordable housing, a decent wage, the right to healthcare, that we pay to care for our people instead of the flattening of Palestinians and oppressed people abroad is not a radical act — _it is basic and core humanity_.
We will send a loud message to President Donald Trump: **his authoritarianism is no good here**.
We will send a message to ICE: that **secret police do not belong here**.
When I think about what is happening throughout our nation today, and what Donald Trump has done in his demolition of the White House — we must let him know:
That house doesn’t belong to him. It belongs to us. **It belongs to the people of this country**.
There has been a day before this presidency, and there will be a day after.
**And it belongs to us**.
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251026.AR4O-m&refcode=em20251026-10073-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10073%2E2526796%2EA4E6xy)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251026.AR4O-m&refcode=em20251026-10073-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=10073%2E2526796%2EA4E6xy)**
Thank you for being a part of this movement.
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20251026?refcode=em20251026-10073-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=10073_2526796_A4E6xy&akid=10073%2E2526796%2EA4E6xy)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20251026-10073-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=10073%2E2526796%2EA4E6xy)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=23&akid=10073%2E2526796%2EA4E6xy) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=24&akid=10073%2E2526796%2EA4E6xy) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=25&akid=10073%2E2526796%2EA4E6xy) | [](https://bsky.app/profile/aoc.bsky.social?t=26&akid=10073%2E2526796%2EA4E6xy)
---|---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.
* [¿Hablas español?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/espanol-2025/?source=em20251026-10073-P_FND_EGR_GRM)

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=10073%2E2526796%2EA4E6xy)

Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress

* * *
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvhsqD6hnNNbCP7JMDskKO3DcwO3FPpifEsk6R5UXJAqdxB9B_-Pj2UssOlSG4Io0evX2PNn_AWmY41uED1Qod-cHbX4srZhtLXhbV-RGgdnleqXbY0q6inrhJWgtOLyVcsyGaoSeD58VH3apxUCsTrLR6UXzp2Ev0RdgyD6Pz7iOxOyxHmgdUNETsv0GQSmuE7v396-3UsxITgqnwDVbv_7Iho-3M7b_ntGmDzNZVTHznItY_9O864dOjjX3yO-F_rLGukkCCA2wrOKLQfDapxQNRv2M-cuOkR4YQHTGKLJP7Sk4VmbKnVBYvs6Gp_WjB0tJZdau7Uqbo6Mt6_SLAedvImoB9fpUEMLB7xwMQVMA/4l3/OBb3xxkiR9e1_Xo2MP8V-w/h2/h001.nMiWoq1Qjy_kXH5T2yAOsIuYdkZ28QQrRVnlXb98Gwo)Friend— They’re calling this race the _duel of Western Prince William._ Me vs. Ian Lovejoy, head-to-head for District 22. And that’s exactly how I see it. This isn’t business as usual. It’s a showdown of ideas, values, and who’s truly on the side of working families. He’s backed by corporate PACs and billionaire money. He’s trying to paint me as an extremist. He’s running ads to drown out our voices. But in a duel, the strongest fighter doesn’t win with outside help — she wins by staying in the ring, landing the blows voters can see, and never backing down. I’ve always believed that public service means showing up — in schools, at kitchen tables, on doorsteps, and in the halls of power. **I’m not here for optics or power plays. I’m here to deliver for you.** But this duel is far from over. And in the final round, every voice, every vote, and every dollar will count. clean_body: | | | — They’re calling this race the duel of Western Prince William. Me vs. Ian Lovejoy, head-to-head for District 22. And that’s exactly how I see it. This isn’t business as usual. It’s a showdown of ideas, values, and who’s truly on the side of working families. He’s backed by corporate PACs and billionaire money. He’s trying to paint me as an extremist. He’s running ads to drown out our voices. But in a duel, the strongest fighter doesn’t win with outside help — she wins by staying in the ring, landing the blows voters can see, and never backing down. I’ve always believed that public service means showing up — in schools, at kitchen tables, on doorsteps, and in the halls of power. I’m not here for optics or power plays. I’m here to deliver for you. But this duel is far from over. And in the final round, every voice, every vote, and every dollar will count. If you believe in fighting for quality education, healthcare access, and dignity for every family — then I’m asking you to stand with me now. ** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. Over the last months, we’ve matched his attacks with truth, we’ve met more neighbors than his corporate money ever could, and we’ve shown that community, sincerity, and integrity still matter. Let’s make the outcome of this duel decisive. Let’s prove that a campaign built on people, not dark money, is the one that wins. Thank you for being in this fight with me. — Elizabeth Guzman | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District. She came to America as a single mother, becoming a union leader and one of the first Latinas in the Virginia General Assembly. She passed stronger gun safety laws, expanded collective bargaining rights and paid sick leave, and protected reproductive rights. To donate by check, send to: Friends of Elizabeth Guzman PO Box 74, Nokesville VA, 20182 \- \- Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman. # **[Campuses cry, 'Save Gaza!' but say NOTHING when Hamas executes Palestinians](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
Since Hamas’ brutal invasion of Israel on October 7, 2023, pro-Palestine protests have erupted across the Western world. Two years in, these riotous events calling for the death of America and Israel, the fall of the West, and jihadi violence continue to rage.But Mark Levin can’t figure something out: If these protesters are so concerned about the Palestinian people, why aren’t they upset about Hamas' recent brutality against Palestinians?Protesters are in the streets screaming about Israel starving Gazans — “a lie,” Levin says — but they don’t bat an eye when Hamas, just hours after releasing hostages as part of the U.S.-brokered ceasefire and peace deal, orchestrates mass public executions of Palestinians.
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.)
[](http://trk.libertyledger._c2VnbWVudD01MzYwMTQifQ/_PJTEAMT7cHPCf2ayko2CQ)
---
[](http://trk.libertyledger.)
# **[Why the Bible is suddenly flying off shelves across America](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
I’ve watched a lot of cultural moments come and go in my lifetime, but this one has felt different. The shocking assassination of Charlie Kirk didn’t just send tremors through the conservative world — it created a ripple that reached far beyond it. In the days and weeks that followed, I saw headlines I never thought I’d see: reports of a Bible-sales surge unlike anything in recent years. When the noise of culture gets stripped away, the hunger for truth rises to the surface.According to Fox Business, more than 2.4 million Bibles were sold in the U.S. in September — a 36% increase over last year.
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.)
[](http://trk.libertyledger.-xQ)

# **[Cities continue to impose, tighten camping bans over concerns about homeless, fires](http://trk.libertyledger.-YN7yH6ykG5YzpWIZ8QIg)**
---
The Washington state city of Tacoma has passed a new ordinance to expand its public camping ban, establishing new “buffer zones” where camping is prohibited around public parks, schools, libraries and permanent shelters. Approval of the ordinance is at least partially a response to the closure of several temporary shelters, which had previously created buffer [...]...Click to read more

# **[If it's 'worse than Watergate,' then why the media blackout?](http://trk.libertyledger.-H5jCQ)**
---
In a sense, this is old news. In December 2021, CNN reported that the House’s January 6 committee had subpoenaed phone records of more than 100 people.But that was mostly Trump officials, including White House Chief of Staff Mark Meadows. No surprise there. After all, the January 6 Select Committee was empaneled for the specific purpose of turning President Donald Trump into a criminal for supposedly aiding and abetting the Jan. 6, 2021, breach of the Capitol.It is well past time for the Republican Congress to fulfill its promise to hold accountable those who weaponized the federal government against Trump and his allies.
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.-H5jCQ)

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1904, the New York City [subway officially opened](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAYBOCnwRsW_o1NPDB08ZauHS1sfuK_xRBhEsTbV4n6Bt1O-qQ76b6uy9WQp3D7NAfq7OISsJwec8b0jvE46Lbn9AgUQMBli87hAgIG3gkOarGGfF1kY7VDBOwb1J0au2GYpBybXbtpEHZSyti1V2oVzkzQq-3S2XVFia4h_WrrBKhOCClkO_SbcohGyotbxbgxKdBSjqn9jhTyjs7X-MTUbvFovN5zvp-s_2AwM5hLKe1OZ46pvpzBnHc6uTWVU-B_wmCOW44rJg6KwRyw8BrTD98oV34-1mIqj3cMZ5O0vRfyns5RMSluhyH_Hs7_euup4TcAAP__eO9_dQ) for service, with Mayor George McClellan piloting the first train on the 9.1-mile route from City Hall to Harlem before opening the 28-station line to the public. ## Gov. Newsom Considering 2028 Presidential Bid
Democratic California Gov. Gavin Newsom indicated in an interview taped last week that he is considering [running for president](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L-O4yAQx_GnwZ0jPAZDCopr3OXaKy0Mg5kTfyybTZS3Xzn7CNuNNNJX-vz-PZaHpURl-2szmhbqUvB1JmwNj89Zc6YttuOrUAEO2tUQcAEOsh94D2rxltJ7EZ03ARFw7NAMahqkvAsxddFoba0CbuV6X13QEx-C1bhaj6NE51xH5qoNHNTAueDiJkenAqIVQoeVT44Jjp5aPW7_K5UWMaR3feJxczV3ycTW9pONfxjMDOYWMVJK14_BHGvGS8FgdjbvlrbCYJZSjUqP_Y-vv1j9fuBJHksjm_qVPIO5O4zfX5QSnZNigm_Z0ifctV8t9TTwHQAA___Y23wY) in 2028, with plans to weigh the decision more seriously after the 2026 midterm elections. When asked whether he was considering a presidential run, Newsom, whose final term as governor ends in January 2027, responded, _“Yeah,[I’d be lying](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpDAMgJ8mdKDEmJ8pUpx0optrr0SZ4IBXIRmFMGjefpXZR9juk2x98uf_9_lu2HNY_5mddHZxDnQdnnKm9MG477xuOZ2BA0gYbXSOZpDQ1UrWMMyLYf-esVq0IwJqK9Jq6FXX3RD7atPgZNt2MCjp4KEWHAc3LjeyD-wWi85UrItNybIiUWLTtXZwRAZxdA_ZW4GSFs4xNV-RQ97I-Xd8UWps3Cuvt5yfh2j_CJgETNd1NW-zxVimAqbSIGAyKbP1VNAazy6mwKZeiybEVP-U1vvp_VGXzFphN6p-lNhsefeindbT2niGTEm0f1WV9PK82Hs--kGgXHfD_nNQ_tUXXxq-AwAA__9nKIVi) otherwise. I’d just be lying. And I’m not—I can’t do that.”_ Newsom has emerged as an [archrival](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGO6yAQQE9jd1h4jMEpKH6TLr_d0sIwhFlhiIDEyu1XjvYGW82TRvP0NF-39WYoUrr_Nzvq5vOa8KgRW8PywbzvdA-tPBMl4LDY7D2uwGFmI2egVmcovlfRO-0RAace9ajkOM8XIWQftL_YadwWaZWzfBknibO_-BnUZGHeHPSkT9vIQY2cCy6GebLKIxohFr9xaTvB0VHLZfjOlFpAH9_5hWWwee-jDq09ajf96-DawfU4jsFu9Uw_9x1cT_wdeWfVvCsLyA6KkdmcKjks7AjUkIX8rMjKMzHjGxYGHCTDiLZRTqejL9o9zkuqUnWC33dD8ZPR_vS7l4afAAAA___4b4OQ) to President Trump in 2025, sparring over the deployment of federal troops to California and calling the president a “wrecking ball” in the interview. Speculation that Newsom might run for president took shape this year after he made visits to multiple Democratic battleground states, including South Carolina, which is slated to hold the first Democratic primary.
## Anonymous Donor of $130 Million Identified
The ally of President Trump who donated $130 million to pay U.S. troops during the government shutdown has been identified as reclusive billionaire [Timothy Mellon](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD0OnDAUhE8DnZF52MAWLtLQbdqUyNjPy4v8g4yBcPuIzRHSjWY0o0_z6z2_NXmKn586oCouzRGv3WMpmL8yhUCfteQjUgQOo0nO4QwcJGs5g2G2mvw9i9oqhwjY1ajaoW-lfAnR16vCxXCJsDj-Gk3XA7S8x06P0r2k7ISsST1rLYeh5Vxw0cjODA5RCzG6hfemEhwtlZSb34liWdH5O52YG5NC7dVayrZX3Y8Kpgqm67oal_486E9ewbQlT4XMXsFU8hE2pr2_mU1RF7Ss7TgL5D2lyDZ9s5JT2namA1m2r0ex6Yos44nao2UZjT92OpEt_zqaMrIVKddZ2e16zL0fKsE_QZP_Epb_uvVU8DcAAP__5nWO1g). Mellon, an heir of the [Mellon banking](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHu4yAMxp8m2VwBIaEZGG7p1ltvjGgxhRPgiDiN-van9B7hv9n65J9-_v7cl7tLOdXXb1fQcqCl4rFlZMb2HamU9Irc9pqqEur6pBBwUUKNIAUos3iX8mfRvbcBUeHQo5VmkuM4az310c7i8RiVmvUsjHTaORTjNA-onRgmJec-2ZMmhTJSCC30ZRyeJiA6ra_hIaZnpwX6xNQufylVjhjyh97YLk8qfbaRed264Venbp26HcdxqZ7fZ3iu1LKH85FO3TgV4viBgjlTBY4Ij5RzoupSQzgigafqGD3IQUD5nwEThL162DfgRrRu4AJjg9cpUQtWhi3u7Omo3_vsgdteVphHaa5X0zfr1-OEbZPptHgVl_JXnn_U-NuqfwEAAP__mS2XMg) and railroad fortune, offered historic donations to Trump's presidential campaign and was also a supporter of Health Secretary Robert Kennedy Jr.'s presidential bid. The anonymous donor's identity was revealed in a _New York Times_ report on Sunday, which cited two people familiar with the gift. The [legality](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMGupCAQRb8Gd3SgRNEFi9m4e7OdpeFJ0TIBymC97vj3E_sXZneSqpzce_98rV8-5VSfv31Bx5HWiu8zIzO2D1Ip6blz-6mpgoJpoxhxBQWD1EqCXYNP-VpNF1xEBOw7dNqOehhmY8ZudxDnMA0W9Owng_1mVejtYL_tHCL4TXXJ3TatwGqljDKPod9sRPTGTPFbjZswCkNiao-_lCrvGPNFL2yPjUqX3c58nKL_JWARsNTroJPvk4DlFgtYtLrZCFh-TnmXErAEPHzjgpUlRfn2TTbcML3wlLpXsqScE1UZGxVZrpOxXTJQpSaZ5OEvyY3ouE1dc-F43__naIVRz-JT_mTj_xr05eBfAAAA__8dF4i6) of whether the Pentagon can disburse the $130 million donation to troops without congressional approval remains a matter of debate.
## Actress June Lockhart Dies at 100
Actress [June Lockhart](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOwyAURE-DOyz4xuAUFNu4y7ZbWhh_4p_FEBkSK7dfOVfY7o1GmhnNz3W6OoqUbt9uQ1tDnhIeJWKtuH8wbxvd1ro_EyUQMPgcAk4goOdScDDT4ii-J9UsNiACdg1aabTs-4tSulltUNr4-XIJwyxnOQzGqAXkMvSd0L3XuiF7pkkBRgqhhGr7zpuA6JQawiy0Z0rgQjXv7T1TqiuG-M4v3FuftybatdZHYd0Xg5HBeBxH6-dyTj99BuOJDMb7MyGP2f-ubq88ulLo1KVySrw8nEeeZ6oMxma3y-OgGKlow5S4bY7ip6z-66GXhb8AAAD__3Zkecc), best known for her roles as the family matriarch in the TV series _Lassie_ and _Lost in Space_ , died last week at the age of 100, according to her family. Lockhart began her career as a child, acting on stage and playing alongside her parents, Gene and Kathleen Lockhart, in a production of _A Christmas Carol_. She won a Tony award for her role in the Broadway comedy _For Love or Money_ , and then rose to [prominence](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOwyAQRL8Gd45gA7ZTUFzjLtdeaTmw2JvDYMEmUf7-5PzCdU-a0dNofq7TdaZIafmeN7Qc8pTwVSMyY_lg3jZaVi6PRAkkDC6HgBNIMK2SLfSTnym-J914GxABzw1a1XfKmIvWXbNadwneozRqMD744GBQl05JMKa_aaWHhuxhUxJ6JaWW-mTOrg-Is9ZDuMnOCS3RE-dyumdKvGKI7_zEcnJ5a6Jdmfcqzl8CRgFjeu-58hEJGA-xgFHJgzsBIybGwjOlDRMLGClV8tjeHwnbmN3vOheubX2UvVCltLQvir49OgLGpli_vyhGql0vtFy2meJnBP_ruaeFvwAAAP__gpuBAA) as the mother in _Lassie_ , the hit TV show about a boy and his border collie. She later played the mother of a family living in a space colony in _Lost in Space_ , a role that led to her becoming a NASA spokeswoman due to the show's influence. See June Lockhart's [life in photos](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2upDAQhE9jMkbtxvwFBJuQvU03RMZuhn5r7JHd8xC3XzFX2OwLqkqf6s_X8mU5cHz-tgdNsqUl0lkCiVD-YDoOfu6S35EjAg4ubRstCNjWGmrsF285XIup_LQRITUVTbrvdNuOxnTVPlmksV-1Hw1oh75dt86s7TDosUHyDVQ83WsasNcABsyjbVy_EVljhm2FzikD5FlSfnwnjrLTFq70Q_nh0lGFaRd5FdX8UjgrnM_zfLyLleTtdQcUzi928s5UP20IlC-FM0WhLJbjQVEUzo4CrZmFqSicbx2Fs4abb7JOMpVSf78j1SG5v7vNUnumUjubiXL92pOkuzt0IxhsG4Be4Vzlyb9ODoFL1ysDz8Ny-GjLf339M-G_AAAA__97_5EK).

Anonymous Donor of $130 Million Identified The ally of President Trump who donated $130 million to pay U.S. troops during the government shutdown has been identified as reclusive billionaire . Mellon, an heir of the and railroad fortune, offered historic donations to Trump's presidential campaign and was also a supporter of Health Secretary Robert Kennedy Jr.'s presidential bid. The anonymous donor's identity was revealed in a New York Times report on Sunday, which cited two people familiar with the gift. The of whether the Pentagon can disburse the $130 million donation to troops without congressional approval remains a matter of debate. Actress June Lockhart Dies at 100 Actress , best known for her roles as the family matriarch in the TV series Lassie and Lost in Space , died last week at the age of 100, according to her family. Lockhart began her career as a child, acting on stage and playing alongside her parents, Gene and Kathleen Lockhart, in a production of A Christmas Carol. She won a Tony award for her role in the Broadway comedy For Love or Money , and then rose to as the mother in Lassie , the hit TV show about a boy and his border collie. She later played the mother of a family living in a space colony in Lost in Space , a role that led to her becoming a NASA spokeswoman due to the show's influence. See June Lockhart's . ## Alamo Trust CEO Ousted, Replaced
The nonprofit that manages the Alamo appointed former Texas Secretary of State Hope Andrade as its [next CEO](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOtCAUhZ8GOwxcUbSg-Bu7-dstzRUuIwmKAXacefuN-wrbfcXJyfnO12N5YIjheP7HnUx9-7QcdJVItVK-ESPuyVJac3BPsmmn4tK1gICeS8FBLw5D_CyqcWYVbpINGakH2Q8wQd9sBhV4C0qJUeje-xHWwXbWa4ejVxLWJpi7TArQUgoQ0I5D76TsoO_GyVk3MiXIhZpyWzeq9Mbi4ye9KLc27U00W61nYd0_BjOD-bqudv8UPPCo6QjpDjGYbysGc0wWI4MZcw020k23IK_5u1S-pZM4Hi6jI24pcZBSTlpN7bmdTTbuvEKMoQyaKfHcMcTfCfVvx70M_AQAAP__0t2CkA) on Friday. Kate Rogers, the Alamo Trust's former CEO, resigned amid criticism over "troubling writings" and a Columbus Day social media post that recognized Indigenous Peoples Day. Texas Land Commissioner Dawn Buckingham denounced the since-deleted post, [calling it](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LtuAyEQheGnYTtbzGBuBUWa7RwpSpFyxTJjG4k11oJvbx9tXiHd0Sm-4v85TseYS76eP-PCob9OdbrysxXunddtxhKXmrjOa6Yzp7pwo_qcUKLegdyhnSjm8p4OA4VZkoeBA1gD2qBHPVxCimr2PslExkpwaSYF4NIpyqjNrNKQw4aBRAsgUeLeGU0ACrVynhI5cZBMudd13y_c-RXbqbzrg9d9qstQwqX3WxPqQ-AocHxtr8Dx-PV9LyU_4lXg2Hrs9yZwBG-dRkSnjbNKK6fcsAa6PXMpuRkrDvK8xFz-6P6_II-AvwEAAP__el9x6g) "misaligned with the culture of The Alamo" and "frankly unacceptable." Last week, Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick called for Rogers to resign over views she expressed in a 2023 dissertation, including her [vision](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLvOnDAQRp8GOiN7uLpwkYbuT5sSDXhgrTUeZE_28vbR7iuk-6rv6Jw_P8sPhhjS8RtPcvLaeUn0LJFEKH8mRjx5I15z8AdtfFLx_FxAQ6-MVjAuHkN8L13t3aq9NTU5Mw6mH8BCX9-cnWDt-30y2reT7dbVjmD3rWsnYzUOYx3c58xoGI3RoKGZht4b00LfTtZvfqo6TT4I50ZuJPTCssc3Pyg3G591dDeRq1TtrwrmCuaCCZNwCpzp4iwN56OC2WNSF0oO211tGGNRO2f1tVOS_xZRdxRSmQ_KRWUq4UgogVMFc52dv54hxlCGser0cWKIX7j8X7KHg38BAAD__wksgg8) for the Alamo "to become a beacon of historical reconciliation." San Antonio Mayor Gina Ortiz Jones and Bexar County Judge Peter Sakai [lamented](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3OqzAMQJ8mbEGJIfwMGe7C9t31jsgQ00YNdZW4pX37K_oK33YkS8c-_vcz_2BM8X75izt5eW883-koiUQon4gJd16JlxzDhVbeqQQ-ZjDgtDUa-jlgTJ-5rYJfTBhtRd72nXUdjOCqq6elGxoyzoy9w2EdsXW23dyyDQC2M1hFf8qsgd5aAwbqoXPB2gZcM4xhDYNqDYUonGu5ktAby5Y-_KJcr7xXyV9FHkU1fxRMCqbjOOpbQTmHCqazRsGUeMWkYDpXKZisOblVMH37tORnEb0Sa36K5k0_uESJfNe4x6AzLc8bFb1l3nURFNKJMFA-1VX24XHElGLpetWay44xfU-T3z305eF_AAAA__-a5YlL) her resignation, calling it "a huge loss for our state" and "gross political interference."
## Paxton Investigating Celina ISD
Attorney General Ken Paxton [announced](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0Dtu6zAQheHVUJ0Nzkh8FSxuo-bCQYoASSeQmrFNgBINkbHl3QfOFtIdnOIr_s_TdAopp_XyFhb2bT-XaeVHzdwab68ZcljKzCVuiS48l4UrlceEEtUB5AHNRCHl5zR05KMkBx17MBqURoequ_qIRp6tNaSi6x2HCCoaSdJp0v3soEv-hYFEAyBR4tFqRQA9qt46msmKQTKlVrZju3LjPdRzfpY7b8e5LF3219ZuVfT_BI4Cx_31Chz_8_oe9lbWjy-BY22hfVeBIzgLtneonB6cHowcVLd5uj1SzqlqIwZ5WULKv3b7W5G7x58AAAD__1e_cZ8) Friday that his office is investigating Celina ISD over its [handling of allegations](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykUL3OpDAQe5rQBSXDb4oU19B9116JQjLASANZwQh23_7EvsLXWbZs2f73M_4EYtqXv2FDL-85jzveJ6MIHg8MHLYcMU8HpQVj3vBM-R7BQKOt0dCNKRB_xrpIfjLJ2QK97VrbtOCgKVbvkot9cjgZFyC2VR2ti13VJDfPdRXagvwTZg101howUPZtk6ytoKl6l2LqVW0wkeSjlBUF3-Gc-ZMvPMqYt4L9KvI6VfVHwaBguO-7jNP5rHh0BcPXomB4KAVDRKY96DlnmQKzjjnEVU_ErJGZsohmDBfquBIn_crHrmm_8BRaglDeFQzF4dPrJmY6207VZtkC8beO_O7Ey8P_AAAA___3cYjW) that former middle school coach Caleb Elliott recorded students in the boys' locker room. "_Any individual who broke the law or was involved in the heinous actions that have been reported will be brought to justice_ ," Paxton said. His announcement follows a call by State Rep. Jeff Leach (R-Plano) for an [investigation](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OgzAQhU9jOiN7-DOFi23osu2WaLAnYO2AI9sKye1X5ApbzeibH733fm7zDQOHY_3GnWx53eN80JmZSqF0tci4R0dxScGv5OJO2cdzBgWd1ErCMHsM_J7byttF-VFXZPXQ666HEbpqs12rBzKmX7zyjbmja0ZD0PUjNaNrnamCvZ5pBYPWChTUpu-81g10jRm980a0inwoMdVlo0IvzHd-xyel2sW9YruV8sii-RIwCZjO86zdki8X11zA9DkRMF3oKjGVTX6gZDx3_KUkHTJniasMx5NyCSuWEA_piMOBMmQvObprMcW4y-zw8MgCpipZ_zgDc8j9IFq17hj4o6v8L82nhb8AAAD__43Si-M)[ into the matter](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OgzAQhU9jOiN7-DOFi23osu2WaLAnYO2AI9sKye1X5ApbzeibH733fm7zDQOHY_3GnWx53eN80JmZSqF0tci4R0dxScGv5OJO2cdzBgWd1ErCMHsM_J7byttF-VFXZPXQ666HEbpqs12rBzKmX7zyjbmja0ZD0PUjNaNrnamCvZ5pBYPWChTUpu-81g10jRm980a0inwoMdVlo0IvzHd-xyel2sW9YruV8sii-RIwCZjO86zdki8X11zA9DkRMF3oKjGVTX6gZDx3_KUkHTJniasMx5NyCSuWEA_piMOBMmQvObprMcW4y-zw8MgCpipZ_zgDc8j9IFq17hj4o6v8L82nhb8AAAD__43Si-M). In response, Moore Middle School Principal Allison Ginn was [placed on nondisciplinary leave](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkb2q3DAQRp9G7maRxuu_wkUadzdtSjPSjNaCsbXI4vru2wfnFdJ98MGBw_nztX5R0nS8ftMuc_2JeT3kOlVqlXJPUtpzkOxL4peEvMvJ-VrRYgfOAg4rU9LP-mx49pYn18jsht51PU7YNdvcxxiF2o6dxCf7rp_6wdqpswM5DhibNN8wZ3FwzqLFx9h37FyLXTtOHHg0Tyucai6PukmVHzqjfvK3lEfIe6PzVuv7NO0vg4vB5bquxxWJ7tPgQqWmoGJwub0MLpoDqcEliKaDYEuvDc6w5awQc66eVCFkChv4pAqimnKt8FYKwpAPOPIBnM6Q3jegfECFvgUoVilw5gOoFDmrwQXHAdj5aXStgzH6AZ5MDJ4dAbfeecHQipemzPy-kmo6-8E87WunpP_k6v8l-Z7xbwAAAP__iCuh1Q), as was Bill Elliott, the alleged perpetrator's father, who also serves as the district's athletic director and high school football coach. The families of three alleged victims [sued the district](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGupDAQRE8DmZHdYAyBg03IZtMNkcfdQEs2HmHPwNx-xb_Cz15QKlW9f4_54Tjwvv51kWy5ljTvdOZApdBxowsuJk_peTCu5FOkjOmcQYIWSgowMzoO37mr0T4ljqomq0yvdA8j6HqzZAavl5bGvjdoDA1DD8qNRmqvh066mu1dpiQYpSRIaIZeo1It6HYY0eNQdZKQSzqaslGhy-UlfNOHjsanWAe7lfLKVfungqmC6TzPZklXd9-4AxVMN1YwfdgXjlnkNwlPgXcnOKPwwXHkfRXIuRzsi9gYhU_Ob1lsnEs6viLT9XZBRM4-7fj2pT4svk4OgXNvqk6u0XH4GVR-p_Fj4X8AAAD__7ANidg) last week, accusing administrators of ignoring or covering up previous instances of criminal predation by Elliott. The lawsuit suggests that the school may have been trying to protect the reputation of its high school football program.
## Lawmakers Ask DOJ to Investigate Smithsonian
U.S. Sens. Ted Cruz and John Cornyn, along with U.S. Rep. Randy Weber (R-Friendswood), are asking the Department of Justice to look into whether the Smithsonian Institution has [violated federal law](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2ynDAMhZ8GOu_Y4ncLijTb3bQpGWELUGJsxtYuS54-w-YRbveNNPp05vz6Gr-QPYflJ240yHuOY6AjexKhdCF63KKlOCV2C9m4UXbxGEFDo4xW0I0O2Z9jXbph0u5uShpM15qmhTs05Tq00LZdZ52uoWqdq2DWYGDuG03N1Nf3kodLZjR0xmjQcOvbxhlTQVP1d2ddX9SaHEtMN1lJ6I159md8UbrZuJV-WEX2XFQ_CngU8DiO45Z3tHRtC3h8WNF79zGhcAzXjAJKTFnZmMIZFAanbHr-Vdbjria0fxQuyCGLyhvLmmNgDOq_Kq9PEU_KccacaZv8ed3xlpVF79Uck3Lxt-Lwoiy8fJ6WaXD7wd5zbrui1suG7D_55Xutvwb4FwAA___huZvc) by using public funds to obstruct the relocation of the space shuttle _Discovery_ to Houston, as mandated by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. The lawmakers accuse the organization of expressing disapproval of the relocation to Capitol Hill staffers, coordinating with the press to generate negative publicity, and disseminating inaccurate information about the logistics and [costs associated with it](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQRb_G7ohgYoNTUGzj7m27pTWGiTMSGAvmxcnfr_J-4XW3uLr36Pz7Wr6QE-_bX8zk5XUvy05nSyRC9RMxYS6Bylo5bhRKphbLuYCGURmtwC0ROb2XoY9-1fFmevLGWTNauMHYPzyEwWnr4jrR6CY3jWBvZrDOrivieHU9-8-Y0eCM0aDhMtkxGnOF8TrdYohTN2iKLKVe5EFCL2z39C5PqpdQcp_8Q-Ro3fVPB3MH83melxaYduE7B8xUOeD-qXYwYxUOiTqYc3nyvqkdG6p2YCDVHt8iiZQU9XOi7rVkFYMK5TtFFTHjRopF0eugKk01fHcw99XH4-SUuFnXDXrLyOmHTH7n8-nhfwAAAP__LKWMLw). Smithsonian officials have stated that relocating the shuttle would cost up to $150 million—far more than the $85 million allocated for the move—and would [require disassembling it](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGu5CAQRE-DM0bQHhs7INjE2d90QwvTzRgJGwt6PzO3X3mv8LOSSnpVen--1i8XUzxfv91Blt8hrye1moiZyh1dckf2lLcS8UU-H1QxtxUUDFIrCWZFF9NnfXZoN4Wz7shqM-phhBmGbrfa6ZnQeBNmDNPm9bAZMm7qEcI0b1sX7Q3TCozWChQ8pnFArXsY-mlGj5N4KsLIuTx4J6a3qyF98jeVh89Hl-zOfFXR_xKwCFhaa48Q3mdudy1guekCFq0ELAoELCc1WY_Ie81ndKfEWMgzoeQsr0KXKyTr5TzJuv9lTiQxVn8PfmTIRRZK2TuO-RSwdMXi1WJKsY5GPNXrcDH9P8Y_0_lt4V8AAAD__64Aizo). The lawmakers' letter disputes those assertions as well as claims that the Smithsonian legally owns the _Discovery_. The Anti-Lobbying Act "prohibits the use of appropriated funds for communications intended to influence members of the public to pressure Congress regarding legislation or appropriations matters," the [letter reads](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0M2OmzAUBeCnMTsj-xLjsPACpWIxavojtZolwr4XYsnYkTGT0Kev0j7C7I7O4ujoe7-O18kHH5dv00qmPOc0RnpsgUqh_IpTmNbkKNnscSGXVtowPUYQoLgUHPSIkw_HeKrQWIGdrMhI3UrVQgequplWIrak9aw7Z09uRqE0OaEbtNSJFipvXmNSgJZSgID63CqUsgHVnDt0eGYnQehLynW5UaHntM3hSB-Ua5fWKphbKfeNNT2DgcHgMNZr2mq3bvW8lz3Tq4lUGAyU-t97_0v_-Hlp9jdFaSa6WgbDJeV4RH7J-x_-TpYy72Px_Guy9vBxYaBA9K7wL9_f-H-b-o5zlQ3eHz4Ev7WancSyTj78O1U-R_lh4G8AAAD__woyiI8). The Smithsonian has not commented on the letter due to the ongoing government shutdown.

Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Alamo Trust CEO Ousted, Replaced The nonprofit that manages the Alamo appointed former Texas Secretary of State Hope Andrade as its on Friday. Kate Rogers, the Alamo Trust's former CEO, resigned amid criticism over "troubling writings" and a Columbus Day social media post that recognized Indigenous Peoples Day. Texas Land Commissioner Dawn Buckingham denounced the since-deleted post, "misaligned with the culture of The Alamo" and "frankly unacceptable." Last week, Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick called for Rogers to resign over views she expressed in a 2023 dissertation, including her for the Alamo "to become a beacon of historical reconciliation." San Antonio Mayor Gina Ortiz Jones and Bexar County Judge Peter Sakai her resignation, calling it "a huge loss for our state" and "gross political interference." Paxton Investigating Celina ISD Attorney General Ken Paxton Friday that his office is investigating Celina ISD over its that former middle school coach Caleb Elliott recorded students in the boys' locker room. "Any individual who broke the law or was involved in the heinous actions that have been reported will be brought to justice ," Paxton said. His announcement follows a call by State Rep. Jeff Leach (R-Plano) for an . In response, Moore Middle School Principal Allison Ginn was , as was Bill Elliott, the alleged perpetrator's father, who also serves as the district's athletic director and high school football coach. The families of three alleged victims last week, accusing administrators of ignoring or covering up previous instances of criminal predation by Elliott. The lawsuit suggests that the school may have been trying to protect the reputation of its high school football program. Lawmakers Ask DOJ to Investigate Smithsonian U.S. Sens. Ted Cruz and John Cornyn, along with U.S. Rep. Randy Weber (R-Friendswood), are asking the Department of Justice to look into whether the Smithsonian Institution has by using public funds to obstruct the relocation of the space shuttle Discovery to Houston, as mandated by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. The lawmakers accuse the organization of expressing disapproval of the relocation to Capitol Hill staffers, coordinating with the press to generate negative publicity, and disseminating inaccurate information about the logistics and . Smithsonian officials have stated that relocating the shuttle would cost up to $150 million—far more than the $85 million allocated for the move—and would . The lawmakers' letter disputes those assertions as well as claims that the Smithsonian legally owns the Discovery. The Anti-Lobbying Act "prohibits the use of appropriated funds for communications intended to influence members of the public to pressure Congress regarding legislation or appropriations matters," the . The Smithsonian has not commented on the letter due to the ongoing government shutdown. | Flying together with our sponsor If you enjoy The Flyover’s quick updates, you’ll love It’s a politically neutral newsletter with summaries of the biggest headlines, paired with biblical reminders to keep your heart grounded in truth. Delivered three times a week, , it’s a reliable way to stay informed while keeping your eyes on Christ. The ship channel is closed as the center span is cut and lowered onto a barge that will transport it to a dock between Portland and Ingleside for recycling. () ➤ Mexico failed to deliver 925,000 of the 1.75 million acre-feet of water owed to the U.S. over the past five years by Friday, causing significant hardship for ranchers and farmers in South Texas. () ➤ Nacogdoches: Police are looking for 17-year-old Hector Cuarenta, who is accused of fatally shooting three teenagers on Friday. He is considered armed and dangerous. () ➤ Laredo: A father and son were arrested by federal agents on Thursday at one of the city's ports of entry for allegedly smuggling over 400 firearms from the U.S. to Mexico. The father, Emilio Ramirez-Cortez, is a lawful permanent resident, and his son, Edgar Ramirez-Diaz, is a U.S. citizen. () ➤ Mission: More than 100 U.S. Coast Guard boats are patrolling 260 miles of the Rio Grande as part of Operation River Wall, an initiative announced by Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem last week. () ➤ Fort Worth: Several police officers and witnesses saved a baby girl pinned under a vehicle during a Thursday rollover crash on I-30. The infant and her mother are hospitalized and expected to make full recoveries. () ➤ Austin: Attorney General Ken Paxton is investigating a local nonprofit and political action committee for "a potentially illegal fundraising scheme" in support of Proposition Q, which would increase the average homeowner's annual property tax bill by $302.14 to address a $33 million budget deficit. name: Scott Presler
subject: Phase 2: Chasing Ballots
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Early Vote Action New Jersey Inc
body:
We have just a few days left

| | | | |
---
Friend, before I get into the latest from New Jersey, I want to _**[thank all of our sponsors](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/boW8/?__vbtrk=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**_ who have stepped up to fund a voter registration staffer or ballot chaser this year.
Because of them, we are tied in the polls and ahead of our projections in New Jersey, and this race is 100% winnable.
Right now, my grassroots team is on the ground chasing mail-in ballots and preparing to launch our massive get out the vote program to **FLIP New Jersey** **from Blue to RED** this November, and we’re having great success.
[SPONSOR A BALLOT CHASER](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/boW8/?__vbtrk=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**Republicans are returning their mail in ballots at a higher rate than Democrats.** This is phenomenal news, but it’s not all good... Democrat voters have requested almost 3 TIMES as many mail-in ballots as Republicans, and they still have more votes “in the bank” than we do.
Just like in Pennsylvania in 2024 for President Trump, this year in New Jersey **_we need to hit our magic number of mail-in and early in person Republican votes cast before election day_** so we can turn out the rest of our voters and win a decisive victory on Election Day.
We truly don’t have a moment to waste... in-person Early Voting begins on October 25th, mail-in voting ends on October 28th, and Election Day is November 4th.
[SPONSOR A BALLOT CHASER](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/boW8/?__vbtrk=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Patriot, the momentum is on our side, and the early returns are looking very good, but we have a lot of work to do in the next few weeks if we’re going to flip the Garden State red.
**We need your help to make the difference.** By sponsoring an Early Vote Action staffer, you’re fueling the grassroots work to flip New Jersey RED in 2025.**[Every new voter we register, every mail-in ballot we chase down, and every Republican we get to the polls brings us one vote closer to victory.](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/boW8/?__vbtrk=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**
**_[Please, take meaningful action today by becoming a sponsor of an Early Vote Action ballot chaser in New Jersey](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/boW8/?__vbtrk=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_.**
_In Liberty,_
**Scott Presler**
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/boW8/?__vbtrk=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzI5NDEzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

# [The Ultimate Game of Chicken](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=177214423&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc3MjE0NDIzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjE1NjY5MTEsImV4cCI6MTc2NDE1ODkxMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.ZG9870K8MfSCbh9oiSgYEGcaXaQ9fSc2TR9N2jlN4gY)

---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=177214423&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc3MjE0NDIzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjE1NjY5MTEsImV4cCI6MTc2NDE1ODkxMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.ZG9870K8MfSCbh9oiSgYEGcaXaQ9fSc2TR9N2jlN4gY)

| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvYW5kcmV3eWFuZy9wL3RoZS11bHRpbWF0ZS1nYW1lLW9mLWNoaWNrZW4_.94-t7hLp2VSJMkWcxiIoK9rzr_16nlTSwS2BIbi8o7c?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/andrewyang/p/the-ultimate-game-of-chicken?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

How will the shutdown end?
The federal government shutdown is now entering its 4th week. It started on October 1st and as of this writing there's little optimism. The record for a shutdown - set during the first Trump administration - is 35 days, which would take us to November 4th.
But the deadline for millions of Americans is a bit earlier: November 1st. This is because SNAP benefits - food stamps - would be cut off for Americans in at least 25 states as of the 1st of the month. This would affect at least 20 million Americans who are teetering on the edge of food scarcity out of the 42 million who use food stamps each month. Some states have already started notifying families that they won't get their usual stipend at the beginning of November to buy groceries. Imagine being a working poor American struggling to get by and then receiving that message.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7ffd414f-eca5-4445-b374-d0a3ecf74ff5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
As you'd imagine, Republicans are eager to pin the upcoming deprivation on Democrats. Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) prodded, "What's it gonna take ... for the Democrats to say, 'Gee, huh, maybe -- maybe people should be able to eat.'"
Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, the Dems' lead decision-maker, puts it back on the Republicans, saying "[The imminent ceasing of food stamps availability] should change Republicans' calculus, that they should sit down and negotiate -- negotiate a way to address this crisis."
This is how bad the polarization is; both parties blame each other while tens of millions of American families wonder how to put food on the table heading into the holidays. Polling shows that Americans are split on who to blame but that there's plenty of blame to go around; three-quarters of Americans think that both parties bear at least 'moderate' responsibility for the shutdown.
That seems right.
One reason the Democrats are willing to run out the clock is that November 1st also marks the date that millions of Americans are expected to have their health care premiums shoot up because of the Republicans' curtailing of Obamacare benefits. Their bet is that Republicans will want to return to the table to keep that from happening.
In any kind of rational world, both sides would broker a deal before the November 1st deadline. Republicans might concede on the extension of healthcare subsidies, in part because several Senators and Donald Trump seem to sense how unpopular a spike in premiums will be. Democrats would be hastening to a resolution because who wants to see poor Americans literally unable to feed themselves?
Do we live in a rational world? Chuck Schumer in March opted to keep the government open because he didn't want Trump to be able to run roughshod on various agencies. But the politics of now demand that he 'fight.'
I was fearful at the beginning of the shutdown because I wasn't sure either side had a way out. For Democrats, it's hard to come back without a meaningful concession on healthcare after all of this bluster. For Trump, his entire persona is to burn the bridges and win at all costs, consequences be damned. The shutdown's direct damage to the economy is around $100 million per day.
As an optimist, I think reason prevails and the shutdown ends by this weekend with Republicans giving the Democrats something they can claim as a concession or the Dems giving in to avoid widespread harm to constituents. But at this point, would any of us be surprised if they claimed a new record and blew past 35 days while millions starved? When politics becomes combat, the people become bystanders and victims. Polarization rises, and the people lose. It should infuriate us and break our hearts.
[Forward](https://substack.com/redirect/ac54975d-b657-4e44-94a7-784d251bacb2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is working to reduce polarization around the country. You know another way to become less inflamed? Get off your phone more! Email [matt@noblemobile.com](https://substack.com/redirect/bc84d512-7050-47a7-8e5b-5abfb55e4c6b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) if you want a few months off from your wireless bill and want to be rewarded for logging off with [Noble Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/f9fedad5-3186-473c-a7b5-5842ef1182be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). clean_body: How will the shutdown end? | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more | | ---|---|--- * | | | Oct 27 | | | | | | | | | | | | How will the shutdown end? The federal government shutdown is now entering its 4th week. It started on October 1st and as of this writing there's little optimism. The record for a shutdown - set during the first Trump administration - is 35 days, which would take us to November 4th. But the deadline for millions of Americans is a bit earlier: November 1st. This is because SNAP benefits - food stamps - would be cut off for Americans in at least 25 states as of the 1st of the month. This would affect at least 20 million Americans who are teetering on the edge of food scarcity out of the 42 million who use food stamps each month. Some states have already started notifying families that they won't get their usual stipend at the beginning of November to buy groceries. Imagine being a working poor American struggling to get by and then receiving that message. | | ---|---|--- As you'd imagine, Republicans are eager to pin the upcoming deprivation on Democrats. Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) prodded, "What's it gonna take ... for the Democrats to say, 'Gee, huh, maybe -- maybe people should be able to eat.'" Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, the Dems' lead decision-maker, puts it back on the Republicans, saying "[The imminent ceasing of food stamps availability] should change Republicans' calculus, that they should sit down and negotiate -- negotiate a way to address this crisis." This is how bad the polarization is; both parties blame each other while tens of millions of American families wonder how to put food on the table heading into the holidays. Polling shows that Americans are split on who to blame but that there's plenty of blame to go around; three-quarters of Americans think that both parties bear at least 'moderate' responsibility for the shutdown. That seems right. One reason the Democrats are willing to run out the clock is that November 1st also marks the date that millions of Americans are expected to have their health care premiums shoot up because of the Republicans' curtailing of Obamacare benefits. Their bet is that Republicans will want to return to the table to keep that from happening. In any kind of rational world, both sides would broker a deal before the November 1st deadline. Republicans might concede on the extension of healthcare subsidies, in part because several Senators and Donald Trump seem to sense how unpopular a spike in premiums will be. Democrats would be hastening to a resolution because who wants to see poor Americans literally unable to feed themselves? Do we live in a rational world? Chuck Schumer in March opted to keep the government open because he didn't want Trump to be able to run roughshod on various agencies. But the politics of now demand that he 'fight.' I was fearful at the beginning of the shutdown because I wasn't sure either side had a way out. For Democrats, it's hard to come back without a meaningful concession on healthcare after all of this bluster. For Trump, his entire persona is to burn the bridges and win at all costs, consequences be damned. The shutdown's direct damage to the economy is around $100 million per day. As an optimist, I think reason prevails and the shutdown ends by this weekend with Republicans giving the Democrats something they can claim as a concession or the Dems giving in to avoid widespread harm to constituents. But at this point, would any of us be surprised if they claimed a new record and blew past 35 days while millions starved? When politics becomes combat, the people become bystanders and victims. Polarization rises, and the people lose. It should infuriate us and break our hearts. is working to reduce polarization around the country. You know another way to become less inflamed? Get off your phone more! Email if you want a few months off from your wireless bill and want to be rewarded for logging off with . This week on the I interview real life magician Blaise Serra on how you become one of the best. is on the way to Brooklyn, Chicago, SF and LA in November in connection with the Scott Galloway podcast tour - see you there! | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Andrew Yang 111 Sutter Street, 7th Floor, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?
